description: A passing DKIM signature signs for the From domain (header.d=example.com) on an otherwise clean, trusted, all-passing message. dkimDomains is [example.com] and dkimDomainMatchesFromDomain is true, so no dkim.domainMismatch signal is emitted. Demonstrates the quiet DKIM-aligned baseline the consistency rule must not disturb.

authentication-results: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

authenticationResults.raw: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com
